name: C/p Xmas Bread Pudding
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove crusts from bread; set aside for another use.
2. Cover bread slices with paper towels and let stand overnight.
3. For custard, in a heavy medium saucepan combine egg yolks, light cream, sugar, and salt. Cook and stir over medium heat until mixture coats a spoon. Remove from heat; cool immediately by placing pan in sink of ice water. Stir for 2 minutes. Add vanilla. Cover surface with plastic wrap.
4. In a small bowl, combine raisins; set aside.
5. Combine cherries.
6. Pour only 2/3 cup warm sherry over raisins; pour remaining sherry over cherries.
7. Fold bread (about 9 cups) into custard until coated.
8. Grease a 6 1/2-cup tower mold (without tube).
9. Drain raisins and cherries, reserving sherry.
10. Arrange 1/4 of cherries in bottom of mold; sprinkle 1/3 cup raisins into mold.
11. Add 1/4 of bread mixture.
12. Sprinkle with 2 tablespoons reserved sherry.
13. Repeat layers 3 times, arranging cherries and raisins near edges of mold.
14. Lightly press last layer with back of spoon.
15. Pour remaining sherry over all.
16. Cover mold tightly with foil. Set mold into a crockpot (3 1/2 or 4 quart) with liner in place. Pour 1 cup water into cooker around mold. Cover; cook on low heat about 5 1/2 hours, or high for 3 hours, or till pudding springs back when touched.
17. Remove mold from crockpot and let stand for 10 minutes. Carefully unmold onto serving platter. Serve warm with sherry sauce.
Sherry Sauce:
1. In a mixing bowl, combine egg yolks, powdered sugar, 2 tablespoons cream sherry, 1/4 teaspoon vanilla.
2. Beat whipping cream until soft peaks form in a small mixing bowl.
3. Gently fold whipped cream into egg yolk mixture.
4. Cover and chill until serving time. Serve warm.
